symptoms.symptom_name: Cluster of three to four right-sided focal motor seizures over ~90 minutes

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Recurrent focal seizures without full recovery over a prolonged period define status epilepticus.
Stroke-like episodes in Sturge-Weber frequently present with focal seizures.

symptoms.symptom_name: Failure to regain consciousness between seizures / profound lethargy

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Non-recovery of consciousness between seizures is a hallmark of status epilepticus (including non-convulsive status).

symptoms.symptom_name: Home rescue benzodiazepine (buccal midazolam) given with failed efficacy

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Continued seizures after first-line benzodiazepine define the cluster as refractory and mandate parenteral second-line therapy.

symptoms.symptom_name: Ongoing rhythmic twitching of the right face and right arm on video

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Directly observed continuing focal motor seizure activity — an active seizure at the time of contact.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known Sturge-Weber syndrome with refractory epilepsy and baseline right hemiparesis

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Structural cortical malformation drives drug-refractory epilepsy and predisposes to prolonged clusters.
Leptomeningeal capillary-venous malformation causes chronic hypoperfusion and stroke-like episodes.

symptoms.symptom_name: New dense right hemiplegia worse than baseline

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Worsening focal deficit raises acute ischaemia; may also be post-ictal (Todd's) paresis — indistinguishable without imaging.
Transient post-ictal worsening of the baseline hemiparesis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tachycardia and tachypnoea with shallow breathing (HR 135, RR 26, SpO2 96%, BP 105/65, Temp 37.5)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Autonomic activation and shallow respiration accompany prolonged seizure activity; clinician-measured — sealed. The AI infers compromise from the caregiver's report of shallow breathing, not a measured value.

symptoms.symptom_name: Left V1 facial port-wine stain and left buphthalmos (Sturge-Weber stigmata)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The V1 port-wine stain with ipsilateral glaucoma/buphthalmos is the classic cutaneous-ocular signature; the port-wine stain is video-observable, the buphthalmos requires examination.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of high fever and neck stiffness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fever and meningism reduces the likelihood of CNS infection as the driver, though it does not exclude it.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Repeated seizures + non-recovery of consciousness + failed first-line rescue is a diagnostic emergency mandating immediate escalation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Hypoglycaemia is a rapidly reversible cause/mimic of prolonged seizures and must be excluded first — done by paramedics/hospital, not the AI Doctor.
To identify an acute ischaemic / stroke-like episode underlying the vascular malformation — a hospital investigation.
To detect ongoing non-convulsive status epilepticus once motor activity settles — hospital-based.
Subtherapeutic maintenance levels or metabolic derangement can precipitate breakthrough status.
No fever or meningism; not first-line and would dangerously delay seizure control — only if CNS infection later suspected.
